# Snarkflix

> First-person film reviews in British English — honest, specific, sometimes snarky. One critic; scores and short summaries are augmented with SnarkAI from the review text.

Snarkflix is a single-author cinema review site. Each review has a canonical URL for citation. Content is written for humans; this file helps AI systems and search understand scope, language, and how to link correctly.

## Site

- [Homepage & archive](https://snarkflix.com/): All reviews, search (`?q=`), categories, about.
- [Atom feed](https://snarkflix.com/feed.xml): Newest reviews (for readers & aggregators).
- [XML sitemap](https://snarkflix.com/sitemap.xml): Every review URL (`/review/{id}`).
- [Image sitemap](https://snarkflix.com/image-sitemap.xml): Key review artwork.

## How to cite a review

Use the review’s canonical URL: `https://snarkflix.com/review/{id}` (numeric id). The same review may load from `?review={id}` on the homepage for legacy/share links; prefer the `/review/` URL for attribution.

## Language & region

- Primary language: **English (British)** — `en-GB`.
- Audience: film readers worldwide; voice and spelling are UK-leaning.

## Topics

Film reviews across genres (action, drama, horror, sci-fi, animation, comedy, musical, adventure). Each piece is labeled with release year, category, and a SnarkAI score derived from the written review.

## Machine-readable rules

- `robots.txt` — crawl policy and sitemap locations.
- JSON-LD on pages — `WebSite`, `Organization`, `Blog` on the homepage; `Review` + `BreadcrumbList` on review views (injected when a review is open).

## Contact / rights

Content © Snarkflix. For permissions or corrections, use the site’s contact channels if listed on [About](https://snarkflix.com/#about).
